ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pljeskavica; closest defensible match is 'beef patty food product' (FOODON_03315595). Pljeskavica is a seasoned, grilled ground beef patty of Balkan origin.
ontology.foodex2_description: Beef meat products — minced/ground beef preparations
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP used as closest defensible anchor for minced/ground beef meat products. No specific pljeskavica entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pljeskavica or equivalent Balkan beef patty preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of pljeskavica is one grilled beef patty (approximately 150–200 g) served with ajvar, chopped raw onions, and a portion of homemade bread.

nutrient_source.title: Pljeskavica (Beef) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Pljeskavica (Beef), cr_id G00010159, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported in HPB entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for ground beef patty, cooked (NDB 13357), proportionally adjusted to match HPB total fat of 6.0 g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not reported by HPB; set to -1 where unavailable.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on beef patty / meat-dominant mixed dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pljeskavica in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 28 based on the closest analogous food category: seasoned ground beef patty with minor carbohydrate contribution from onion and bread crumbs. Meat-dominant dishes with low carbohydrate content consistently yield GI values in the 20–35 range in peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (28 × 8.38) / 100 ≈ 2.4 for a 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 369 mg/100g from seasoning and added salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pljeskavica to occasional consumption and avoid adding salty condiments such as ajvar or kajmak, as a 200 g serving delivers approximately 738 mg sodium — nearly half the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4.19 g/100g), low sugar (1.45 g/100g), high protein (18.48 g/100g), estimated GI 28
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pljeskavica is a protein-dominant, low-carbohydrate food with an estimated low GI of 28, making it suitable for glycaemic management in Type 2 Diabetes when served without white bread and with portion-controlled accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Red meat classification, saturated fat (~2.4 g/100g), cholesterol 52 mg/100g, sodium 369 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: As a red meat preparation, pljeskavica should be limited to 1–2 servings per week for individuals with cardiovascular disease, with preference for leaner preparations and avoidance of high-sodium condiments to manage combined saturated fat and sodium burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High arachidonic acid content from ground beef amplifying TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ground beef patties are a consistent dietary trigger for rheumatoid arthritis flares due to high arachidonic acid content; individuals with RA should limit consumption to rare occasions and consider mixed-meat alternatives with chicken or turkey to reduce pro-inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pljeskavica (Beef) is a traditional Balkan grilled meat patty originating from Serbia and the former Yugoslav region, made from seasoned ground beef mixed with finely chopped onion and spices, then grilled over charcoal to produce a smoky, juicy, slightly charred patty. It is typically served with ajvar (a roasted red pepper and eggplant relish), raw chopped onions, and homemade flatbread or lepinja. The texture is dense and meaty with a crisp exterior from charcoal grilling. Nutritionally, pljeskavica is a protein-dominant food providing 18.48 g protein per 100 g, with moderate fat (6.0 g/100g) and low carbohydrate (4.19 g/100g), yielding 148 kcal/100g. Sodium content is notable at 369 mg/100g due to seasoning. It has an estimated low glycemic index of approximately 28, making it suitable for glycaemic management in Type 2 Diabetes when served without high-carbohydrate accompaniments. As a red meat preparation, it carries moderate cardiovascular and hypertension concern due to saturated fat and sodium content. It is not suitable for vegetarian, vegan, or kosher diets. In Singapore, pljeskavica appears in Balkan and Eastern European specialty restaurants and is classified under the HPB Foodo database as a beef-based dish.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010159) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central ground beef patty data proportionally adjusted to HPB total fat. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature as no direct measurement exists for pljeskavica.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific pljeskavica preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010159) or available in USDA FoodData Central for an exact match as of 2026. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 2.4 g/100g from USDA FoodData Central ground beef patty cooked (NDB 13357), proportionally scaled to HPB total fat of 6.0 g/100g. Cultural classification corrected from input 'Middle Eastern' to 'Balkan / Serbian' — pljeskavica is a dish of Serbian and former Yugoslav origin, not Middle Eastern. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for pljeskavica exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. Halal status not confirmed; pljeskavica may be prepared with pork in traditional Balkan contexts, but this record specifies beef only — halal certification depends on preparation facility.
